preamble: The transformation of the voiced pharyngeal fricative [ʕ] (the letter ‘ayn) into the voiceless uvular fricative [χ]. This shift involves both devoicing and a slight retraction of the place of articulation.

examples.note: Maltese 'għ' is realized as [ħ] or [χ] word-finally or before voiceless consonants due to final-obstruent devoicing.
Isolated from the mainland, pharyngeals in this dialect often shift to uvulars or are lost.

sources: Aquilina, J. (1959). The Structure of Maltese.
